displayName: Statement of Reasons Issued to User (notarized, Art.17)
description: Notarize-only record that the platform delivered a DSA Art.17 statement of reasons to the affected recipient — and WHAT it said. Binds the exact statement text to the enforcement decision: `statementHash` is sha256 of the canonicalized statement-of-reasons document actually delivered, so 'did the user get the same reasons the platform decided on (and later submitted to the regulator)?' is cryptographically answerable. Pairs with dsa-transparency-submission-v1 — the user-facing statement and the regulator submission should carry the SAME reasons; divergence between this record's statementHash and the submission's payload is detectable on-chain. Pure notarization: terminal RECORDED in one round-trip. AGLedger attests delivery + content as asserted by the platform; it does not inspect the statement's adequacy.

contentRef.description: Platform identifier of the content/account the statement concerns.

enforcementRef.description: recordId of the enforcement-decision-v1 this statement explains (chain lineage).

statementHash.description: sha256:<hex> of the exact, canonicalized statement-of-reasons document delivered to the user. Binds the disclosure to specific bytes.

deliveryChannel.description: How the statement was delivered. not_deliverable records a justified inability to reach the recipient (e.g. account already removed by the user) — DSA Art.17 applies 'where the relevant electronic contact details are known'.

recipientRef.description: Optional platform identifier of the recipient (pseudonymous). Kept opaque; PII stays off-chain.

redressInformationIncluded.description: Optional attestation that the statement included the required redress-possibility information (Art.17(3): internal complaint, out-of-court settlement, judicial remedy).

deliveredAt.description: Optional: when the statement was delivered to the recipient.
